title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 16 - Mission Packs - Part 1
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=a65XoIF7K7Y

Daniel (@dungeondive) begins a two-part survey of the Shadows of Brimstone mission packs—expansions that sit between deluxe enemy sets and big-box expansions, each containing a new enemy with miniatures, a themed objective room tile, four missions, and a stack of cards. This first instalment covers the Order of the Crimson Hand and the Werewolf Feral Kin.

The Crimson Hand pack brings a secret cult with roots stretching across Flying Frog's game universe (Fortune and Glory, A Touch of Evil), complete with mine encounters, themed artifacts, an Inquisitor super-enemy, and a four-mission mini-campaign. Daniel recommends keeping mission pack encounters separate rather than diluting them into the main deck. The Werewolf pack introduces feral lycanthropes that scale with hero level, a curse mechanic that can transform your hero into a werewolf, silver bullet ammo for gunslingers, and another four linked missions. Daniel considers these two the strongest mission packs, particularly for the hero transformation mechanics they introduce.

----

Would you rather face the Crimson Hand cult's conspiracies or risk a werewolf bite for the power of lycanthropy?
